Bridge diagnostics

★ marks the production-deployed spec per commodity (best of the candidate set + combinations by backtest RMSE). R² is in-sample, R²_oos is walk-forward out-of-sample. p(β_lag4=1) is the Wald-test p-value for the YoY-on-YoY restriction (NA for bojo, which imposes it; equal_avg and inv_mse are combination forecasts and have no in-sample β). RMSE/naive < 1 means the spec beats the seasonal random walk.

Methodology

Production equation per commodity

These are the actual equations driving the headline nowcast above. They’re picked per commodity by walk-forward backtest RMSE from a bench of {aggregate, midas, bojo, equal_avg, inv_mse} specs.

Iron ore — bojo spec

\[\Delta_{4}\log V_{Q} \;=\; 0.009 \;+ 0.502 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q} \;+\; \varepsilon_{Q}\]

Wald test of \(\beta_{lag4}=1\): aggregate p = 0.36, midas p = 0.71. Restriction not rejected; pure YoY-on-YoY wins the OOS leaderboard at RMSE = 4.49 Mt (0.78× naive).

Coal — aggregate spec

\[\log V_{Q} \;=\; 1.196 \;+ 0.774 \,\Delta_{4}\log T_{Q} \;+ 0.732 \,\log V_{Q-4} \;+\; \varepsilon_{Q}\]

Wald test of \(\beta_{lag4}=1\): aggregate p = 0.00, midas p = 0.00. Restriction rejected; the free seasonal-anchor coefficient is doing real work (β₄ ≈ 0.73). OOS RMSE = 2.14 Mt (0.50× naive).

Conventions

  • \(V_{Q}\) is DISR Resources & Energy Quarterly Table 16 physical tonnage (Mt) for the commodity. Coal is metallurgical + thermal summed.
  • \(T_{Q}\) is PortWatch AIS daily tonnage summed across the commodity’s ports over quarter \(Q\). The current-quarter partial-month scale-up anchors on the data’s latest observation date (not Sys.Date()), so PortWatch’s 7–14 day refresh lag doesn’t bias the estimate down.
  • \(\Delta_{4}\) is the year-on-year log difference. The YoY transform strips deterministic seasonality, so no explicit STL / X-13 step is needed at our sample size (n ≈ 23 quarters).
  • Newey-West HAC standard errors with lag = 1. Uncertainty bands from residual bootstrap, scaled by \(\sqrt{1 - \text{share\_observed}}\) so they collapse as the current quarter fills in.
  • Full derivation, candidate bench, and the literature this draws on: docs/METHODOLOGY.md.
